frameworks.name: sustained delay activity
frameworks.one_line: Traditional view that maintained elevated activity supports working memory

frameworks.name: transient delay activity
frameworks.one_line: Time-limited early delay-period activity as the substrate for WM maintenance

frameworks.name: temporal-spectral evolution
frameworks.one_line: Time-frequency decomposition of delay-period EEG across the maintenance interval

frameworks.name: Sternberg working memory paradigm
frameworks.one_line: Probe-recognition task structuring the delay-period analysis

core_question: Is visual working memory delay-period EEG activity sustained or transient, and does it predict subsequent memory performance?
key_assumptions: Delay-period activity indexes working memory maintenance
High-density EEG sensor and source signals can dissociate transient from sustained components
Novel scene stimuli engage visual working memory
manipulations: Six-second delay period in a Sternberg task
Time-frequency segmentation across early vs later delay windows
key_findings: Delay-period activity was transient rather than sustained
High-amplitude theta-range activity appeared in the first three seconds of the delay
Early delay activity correlated positively with subsequent new-versus-old probe discrimination
A right parietal source of transient early activity tracked working memory performance
interpretations: Transient early delay activity, not sustained activity, supports visual working memory
Right parietal theta contributes to scene working memory maintenance
